label: DPP Schema Version
definition: Identifies the version of the schema specification that this Digital Product Passport instance conforms to.
shortDefinition: Identifies the version of the schema specification that this Digital Product Passport instance conforms to.
sourceShortDefinition: The reference standard the DPP instance schema refers to
regulatoryRequirement: The reference standard the DPP instance schema refers to.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The DPP schema version should refer to the required standards for DPPs in the EU that apply to the DPP at hand.
The first version should refer to the standards introduced by JTC-24 when first published in the Official Journal of the EU.

Note:
It may also refer to versions of a future reference dictionary of a battery passport, which is not defined at this moment but indicated as a requirement in JTC-24 draft standards.

label: DPP Status
definition: Indicates the current publication or lifecycle status of the Digital Product Passport record.
shortDefinition: Indicates the current publication or lifecycle status of the Digital Product Passport record.
sourceShortDefinition: The current status of the DPP as a digital resource
regulatoryRequirement:
The status of the DPP instance as digital resource.
Example: "Active", "Archived"
dinSpecRecommendation:
The options for DPP status should be defined as
- Active: current version of the battery passport
- Archived: past version of the battery passport, archived after an update.
- Inactive: A battery passport linked to a new battery passport, which has a different DPP status than 'Inactive', after reuse, repurposing, remanufacturing.
- Marked-for-deletion: a battery passport that has been marked for deletion after recycling or export of the battery

label: DPP Granularity
definition: Describes the level of detail at which passport data is reported, such as model-level or individual unit-level granularity.
shortDefinition: Describes the level of detail at which passport data is reported, such as model-level or individual unit-level granularity.
sourceShortDefinition: The required level of information with regard to product item, batch or model
regulatoryRequirement:
The level of granularity of the ProductID as per ESPR: model, batch or item
In the Battery passport the value shall be 'Item' as each battery will have an individual battery passport.

label: Date-time of Latest DPP Update
sourceAttributeName: Date-time of latest update of DPP
definition: The date and time at which the most recent change to this Digital Product Passport was recorded.
shortDefinition: The date and time at which the most recent change to this Digital Product Passport was recorded.
sourceShortDefinition: Date and time of the latest update of the battery passport
updateRequirement: upon any update of the battery passport
regulatoryRequirement: The date and time of the latest update to the DPP instance.
dinSpecRecommendation:
This timestamp should reflect updates of mandatory and voluntary data in the battery passport.

This time stamp should reflect the date-time of external DPP availability, i.e. when the last update of the DPP has been first made available to third parties.

Note: A time stamp, which may reflect date-time when an information in the DPP has been generated, should be provided as meta data to each data attribute.

label: Unique Battery Passport Identifier
sourceAttributeName: Unique battery passport identifier / unique DPP identifier
definition: A globally unique, persistent URI that unambiguously identifies this specific Digital Product Passport instance.
shortDefinition: A globally unique, persistent URI that unambiguously identifies this specific Digital Product Passport instance.
sourceShortDefinition: The unique identifier of a battery passport, equivalent to the unique DPP identifier in JTC-24 draft standards
updateRequirement: upon any update of the battery passport that includes archiving
regulatoryRequirement:
The unique battery passport identifier / unique DPP identifier serves distinction and identification of archived versions and should resolve to one specific version of a battery passport.

Note: The JTC-24 standards foresee an archiving function for DPPs after updates (exceptions to dynamic data may apply).

Note: A new battery passport version after an update will obtain a new unique battery passport identifier / unique DPP identifier. The previous unique battery passport identifier / unique DPP identifier will be archived together with its previous battery passport version. The initial unique battery passport identifier / unique DPP identifier, likely to be stored in the EU DPP registry, therefore always resolves to the first version of a battery passport.

label: Unique Battery Identifier
sourceAttributeName: Unique battery identifier / unique product identifier
definition: A globally unique URI that distinctly identifies the physical battery unit or model described by this passport.
shortDefinition: A globally unique URI that distinctly identifies the physical battery unit or model described by this passport.
sourceShortDefinition: The unique identifier of the battery item, equivalent to the unique product identifier in JTC-24 draft standards
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include information identifying the battery. The battery passport must be accessible through the QR code […] which links to a persistent unique battery identifier. The battery identifier is a unique string of characters for the identification of batteries that also enables a link to the battery passport.

The battery identifier must comply with ISO/IEC 15459 1:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 2:2015, ISO/IEC 15459 3:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 4:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 5:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 6:2014, or their equivalent.

It shall be based on a URI/URL structure.
Note: According to JTC-24 prEN_18219 (4.) describes the unique product identifier (here: unique battery identifier  shall follow the following principles: (4.1) globally uniqueness, (4.2) persistence, (4.3) syntax, (4.4) semantic, (4.5) Interoperability and (4.6) Openness.

The economic operator placing the battery on the market must attribute the battery identifier to it.

Note: The unique battery identifier / unique product identifier must also be provided on the battery label. Where the size or nature of the battery does not allow it, the required information must be provided on the packaging or in a document accompanying the battery.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The unique battery identifier / unique product identifier may include the information requirement on the battery model identifier as well as that of the battery batch or serial number, or product number or another element allowing the battery's identification.

Such information shall be clearly distinguishable in the chosen format of the identification scheme.

label: Battery Model Identifier
definition: A manufacturer-assigned identifier that designates a particular battery model or product line.
shortDefinition: A manufacturer-assigned identifier that designates a particular battery model or product line.
sourceShortDefinition: The unique identifier of the battery model.
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery model identifier is a shared property of batteries that belong to the same battery model, which is a version of a battery all units of which share the same technical characteristics relevant for the requirements of the Battery Regulation on sustainability, safety, labelling,marking and information.


Note: The model identifier is a unique string of characters and required in the Draft for the CF declaration IA.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The model identifier should be provided to allow attributing data on model level in the battery passport to the specific battery model.

The model identifier may be part of the unique battery identifier / unique product identifier, if clearly distinguishable in the chosen format of the identification scheme.

The model identifier may include the information requirement on the battery serial number, including identification of the the battery batch, if clearly distinguishable in the chosen format of the identification scheme.

Note: Independently of the battery passport, batteries to be placed on the market must bear a model identification.

label: Battery Serial Number
definition: A unique alphanumeric code assigned to an individual battery unit for traceability purposes.
shortDefinition: A unique alphanumeric code assigned to an individual battery unit for traceability purposes.
sourceShortDefinition: Serial number of the battery including information on the battery batch
regulatoryRequirement:
Note: The following requirements of the battery regulation, not directly addressing the battery passport, should be considered:
Manufacturers shall ensure that batteries which they place on the market bear a model identification and batch or serial number, or product number or another element allowing their identification. Where the size or nature of the battery does not allow it, the required information shall be provided on the packaging or in a document accompanying the battery.
The Declaration of Conformity requires the following information (among other information): "Battery model (product, category, and batch or serial number):"
dinSpecRecommendation:
The serial number of the battery should be included to enable identification on batch and item level to attribute reporting requirements on that level to the respective batch or item (as column T describes for each data attribute).

The serial number should include identification of the battery batch, clearly distinguishable in the format of the identification scheme.

The serial number including the battery batch identification may also be part of unique battery identifier / unique product identifier or the battery model identifier, if clearly distinguishable in the chosen format.

label: Unique Economic Operator Identifier
definition: A unique identifier assigned to the economic operator responsible for placing the battery on the market.
shortDefinition: A unique identifier assigned to the economic operator responsible for placing the battery on the market.
sourceShortDefinition: The unique identifier of the economic operator
accessRights: Notified bodies, market surveillance authorities and the Commission
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include an operator identifier. The operator identifier is a unique string of characters for the identification of actors involved in the value chain of products (see ESPR, Article 2(31)). It corresponds to the economic operator responsible for creating or updating the battery passport.

Where a unique operator identifier is not yet available, “the economic operator that creates or updates the digital product passport shall request a unique operator identifier on behalf of the relevant actor.” (see ESPR, Article 12(2)).

The unique operator identifier must, where relevant for the products concerned, comply with ISO/IEC 15459 1:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 2:2015, ISO/IEC 15459 3:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 4:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 5:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 6:2014, or their equivalent until referenced harmonised standards are listed in the Official Journal of European Union (OJEU). (see also ESPR Annex III)
dinSpecRecommendation: When necessary, [an] economic operator [...] is assigned a globally unique economic operator identifier [...]. (see JTC-24 prEN_18219)
sourceRegulationReference:
ESPR Art. 2 (31), 12 (2); Annex XIII (1a) Annex VI Part A; Art. 3(66); Art. 77 (3)
JTC-24 prEN_18219 (4.1.1.1) (draft)
regulationReferences: Annex XIII (1a) Annex VI Part A

label: Unique Manufacturer Identifier
definition: A unique identifier assigned to the legal entity that manufactured the battery.
shortDefinition: A unique identifier assigned to the legal entity that manufactured the battery.
sourceShortDefinition: The unique identifier of the manufacturer of the battery.
accessRights: Notified bodies, market surveillance authorities and the Commission
regulatoryRequirement: The unique manufacturer identifier must, where relevant for the products concerned, comply with ISO/IEC 15459 1:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 2:2015, ISO/IEC 15459 3:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 4:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 5:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 6:2014, or equivalent European or international standards until referenced harmonised standards are listed in the OJEU (see also ESPR Annex III).
dinSpecRecommendation:
Information related to the manufacturer, such as its unique operator identifier and the information referred to in Article 27(7) must or can be specified (see ESPR Annex III (g)).

When necessary, [an] economic operator [...] is assigned a globally unique economic operator identifier [...]. (see JTC-24 prEN_18219)
sourceRegulationReference: Annex VI Part A (1); Art. 38(7); ESPR Art. 2(32); ESPR Art. 12(1); ESPR Annex III

label: Unique Facility Identifier
definition: A unique identifier for the manufacturing facility or plant where the battery was produced.
shortDefinition: A unique identifier for the manufacturing facility or plant where the battery was produced.
sourceShortDefinition: The unique identifier of a facility
accessRights: Notified bodies, market surveillance authorities and the Commission
regulatoryRequirement:
A unique facility identifier is a unique string of characters for the identification of locations or buildings involved in a product’s value chain or used by actors involved in a product’s value chain. (JTC-24 prEN_18219 (3.20))

The unique facility identifiers shall, where relevant for the products concerned, comply with standards ISO/IEC 15459-1:2014, ISO/IEC 15459-2:2015, ISO/IEC 15459-3:2014, ISO/IEC 15459-4:2014, ISO/IEC 15459-5:2014 and ISO/IEC 15459-6:2014. (ESPR Annex III)
dinSpecRecommendation:
A unique facility identifier can be used for the unique identification of the manufacturing place.

When necessary, facility is assigned a globally unique facility identifier. (JTC-24 prEN_18219 (4.1.1))
sourceRegulationReference: JTC-24 prEN_18219 (3.20) (draft); JTC-24 prEN_18219 (draft); ESPR Annex III

label: Economic Operator Information
definition: Contact and descriptive details about the economic operator responsible for the battery's market placement.
shortDefinition: Contact and descriptive details about the economic operator responsible for the battery's market placement.
sourceShortDefinition: Information related to the economic operator
dinSpecRecommendation:
If different from the manufacturer, information on the economic operator should be included:
- name
- registered trade name or registered trademark;
- postal address, indicating a single contact point;
- web address, if available; and
- e-mail address, if available.

The contact details must be indicated in a language or languages which can be easily understood by end-users and market surveillance authorities.

label: Manufacturer Information
definition: Name, address, and contact details for the company that manufactured the battery.
shortDefinition: Name, address, and contact details for the company that manufactured the battery.
sourceShortDefinition: Information related to the manufacturer of the battery
sourceRegulationReference: Annex VI Part A (1); Art. 3, 1(33); Art. 38(7)

label: Manufacturing Place
definition: The geographic location, including country and optionally city or region, where the battery was manufactured.
shortDefinition: The geographic location, including country and optionally city or region, where the battery was manufactured.
sourceShortDefinition: The place of manufacture of the battery
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include the manufacturing place (geographical location of a battery manufacturing plant). It must be indicated as town, region and country/jurisdiction.

The manufacturing place must also be provided on the battery label.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The manufacturing place should be uniquely identifiable. A unique facility identifier can be used for this purpose.

The address of the manufacturing place (country, city, street, or even building if several facilities operate under one address) should be provided in text form.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (1a); Annex VI, Part A(3); Labelling IA (draft)

label: Manufacturing Date
definition: The year and month in which the battery was manufactured, expressed in YYYY-MM format.
shortDefinition: The year and month in which the battery was manufactured, expressed in YYYY-MM format.
sourceShortDefinition: The date of manufacture of the battery
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include the date of manufacture (month and year).

The manufacturing date must also be provided on the battery label.

The manufacturing date must consist of the month and year of the date of manufacture.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The manufacturing date should not only relate to the battery model, but to the battery item.

The date code should comply with DIN ISO 8601 1:2020 12 and ISO 8601 2:2019.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (1a); Annex VI Part A (4); Annex VII Part B (1); Labelling IA (draft)

label: Date of Putting Battery into Service
attributeName: Date of Putting Battery into Service
sourceAttributeName: Date of putting the battery into service
definition: The year and month the battery was first placed into operational service or commissioned.
shortDefinition: The year and month the battery was first placed into operational service or commissioned.
sourceShortDefinition: The date of first use, for its intended purpose, in the European Union, of a battery, without having been previously placed on the market
regulatoryRequirement: Where appropriate, the battery passport must include information on the date of putting the battery into service.

label: Warranty Period
definition: The duration covered by the manufacturer's warranty for the battery, expressed as an end date in YYYY-MM format.
shortDefinition: The duration covered by the manufacturer's warranty for the battery, expressed as an end date in YYYY-MM format.
sourceShortDefinition: Commercial warranty period for the battery
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information about the period for which the commercial warranty applies.

label: Battery Category
definition: The regulatory classification of the battery such as portable, light means of transport, industrial, or electric vehicle.
shortDefinition: The regulatory classification of the battery such as portable, light means of transport, industrial, or electric vehicle.
sourceShortDefinition: Description of the scope of application of a battery in terms of its use according to the battery regulation
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include the battery category.

The battery category must be provided on the battery label.

The battery must be categorised by its intended use in:
- LMT battery;
- electric vehicle battery; or
- industrial battery (stationary battery energy storage system/other industrial batteries).
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (1a); Annex VI Part A (2)

label: Battery Mass
definition: The total mass of the complete battery pack or system as supplied, measured in kilograms.
shortDefinition: The total mass of the complete battery pack or system as supplied, measured in kilograms.
sourceShortDefinition: The mass of the battery
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include the battery weight.
Note: In all other instances we use the term mass instead of weight, which is used in the regulation: Regulatory requirements define the unit as kg or g per regulation and the physically correct term is then mass.

The battery mass must also be provided on the battery label.
dinSpecRecommendation: The mass of  modules and/or cells should also be specified.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (1a); Annex VI Part A (5); Labelling IA (draft)

label: Battery Status
definition: The current lifecycle stage of the battery, such as original use, repurposed, remanufactured, or end-of-life.
shortDefinition: The current lifecycle stage of the battery, such as original use, repurposed, remanufactured, or end-of-life.
sourceShortDefinition: Current status of the battery item in its life cycle
updateRequirement: upon placement on market and battery status change
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include information on the status of the battery.

The status of the battery must be defined as ‘original’, ‘repurposed’, ‘re-used’, ‘remanufactured’ or ‘waste’.

A new battery passport must be issued when a battery was subject to remanufacturing, repurpose or one of the treatment operations preparing for re-use and preparing for repurpose and is placed on the market again.
dinSpecRecommendation:
Additionally to the battery status, the battery’s repair history shall be included.

The repair of a battery shall be made transparent and be recorded as “repaired” under the battery status “re-used”.

label: Separate Collection Symbol
definition: A URI referencing the mandatory symbol indicating that the battery must be collected separately from general household waste.
shortDefinition: A URI referencing the mandatory symbol indicating that the battery must be collected separately from general household waste.
sourceShortDefinition: Symbol indicating the necessity of separate collection
sourceCategory: Symbols, labels and documentation of conformity
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must include the symbol for separate collection.

All batteries must be marked on the label with the symbol for separate collection from 18 August 2025.

The separate collection symbol must cover at least 3 % of the area of the largest side of the battery with a maximum size of (5 × 5) cm2.

For Cylindrical battery cells: The symbol must cover at least 1,5 % of the surface area of the battery and with a maximum size of (5 × 5) cm2.

Where the symbol would be smaller than (0,47 × 0,47) cm2, the battery does not need to be marked with it. Instead, the symbol measuring at least (1 × 1) cm2 must be printed on the packaging.
dinSpecRecommendation: The symbol should be translated into text to ensure machine readability.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (1s); Art. 13(4);  Annex VI Part B

label: Cadmium and Lead Symbols
definition: Chemical hazard symbols or textual indicators that must appear on batteries containing cadmium or lead above threshold concentrations.
shortDefinition: Chemical hazard symbols or textual indicators that must appear on batteries containing cadmium or lead above threshold concentrations.
sourceShortDefinition: Representation of the chemical symbol “Cd” for cadmium and “Pb” for lead contained in the battery
sourceCategory: Symbols, labels and documentation of conformity
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must include information regarding the prevention and management of waste batteries including the meaning of the labels and symbols on batteries or printed on their packaging or in the documents accompanying batteries.

Batteries containing more than 0,002 % cadmium or more than 0,004 % lead, must be marked with the chemical symbol for the metal concerned: Cd or Pb.

The symbols for cadmium and lead must also be provided on the battery label.

The relevant chemical symbol indicating the heavy metal content must be printed beneath the separate collection symbol and must cover an area of at least one-quarter the size of that symbol.
dinSpecRecommendation: The symbol should be translated into text to ensure machine readability of the battery passport.

label: Carbon Footprint Label
definition: The label and corresponding performance class letter assigned to the battery based on its lifecycle carbon footprint intensity.
shortDefinition: The label and corresponding performance class letter assigned to the battery based on its lifecycle carbon footprint intensity.
sourceShortDefinition: Label indicating the carbon footprint and carbon footprint performance class
sourceCategory: Symbols, labels and documentation of conformity
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
dinSpecRecommendation:
The carbon footprint label should be displayed directly in the battery passport in addition to the data in the documentation.

The label should be translated into text/numbers to ensure machine readability of the battery passport.

For the content and calculation of the carbon footprint, see 6.3.2 "Battery carbon footprint per Functional Unit" and 6.3.4 "Contribution of main product production/manufacturing lifecycle stage".
sourceRegulationReference: Article 7(2) via Annex XIII (1c); Labelling IA (draft) Annex V

label: Extinguishing Agent
definition: The recommended fire suppression agent or method to be used in the event of a battery fire.
shortDefinition: The recommended fire suppression agent or method to be used in the event of a battery fire.
sourceShortDefinition: Specification of the usable extinguishing agent
sourceCategory: Symbols, labels and documentation of conformity
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include the usable extinguishing agent or if relevant, the fire class of that agent determined in accordance with standard EN 2:2005

The usable extinguishing agent must also be provided on the battery label.
dinSpecRecommendation:
Where applicable, the extinguishing agent category should be indicated.

The information on the usable extinguishing agent should be related to their possible area of application as regulated in DIN EN 2.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex VI Part A (9); Labelling IA (draft)

label: Meaning of Labels and Symbols
definition: Explanatory text describing what each label, marking, or symbol printed on the battery or its packaging signifies.
shortDefinition: Explanatory text describing what each label, marking, or symbol printed on the battery or its packaging signifies.
sourceShortDefinition: Brief and easily understandable explanatory sentence conveying the meaning of symbols and labels used
sourceCategory: Symbols, labels and documentation of conformity
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must include information regarding the prevention and management of waste batteries including the meaning of the labels and symbols on batteries or printed on their packaging or in the documents accompanying batteries.

The information must be communicated in (a) language(s) easily understood by end-users.

All labels and symbols used in the battery label must be accompanied by a brief and easily understandable explanatory sentence.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The meaning of labels and symbols shall be provided on the battery label or shall be included in the documentation accompanying the battery.

The meaning of all symbols and labels on the battery or/and in the battery passport should be explained.

The explanatory texts should be agreed on across industry to ease the implementation and ensure comprehensibility for the public.

label: EU Declaration of Conformity
definition: A URI linking to the official EU Declaration of Conformity document certifying compliance with applicable regulations.
shortDefinition: A URI linking to the official EU Declaration of Conformity document certifying compliance with applicable regulations.
sourceShortDefinition: The document that declares compliance with requirements for placement on EU market
sourceCategory: Symbols, labels and documentation of conformity
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include the EU declaration of conformity.

The regulation specifies the following requirements regarding the EU declaration of conformity (see BattReg Annex XIII (1r) and BattReg Article 18 (2)):

- The EU declaration of conformity has the model structure set out in Annex IX.
- The EU declaration of conformity must contain the elements specified in the relevant modules set out in Annex VIII.
- The EU declaration of conformity must be kept up to date.
- It is translated into the language or languages required by the Member State in which the battery is placed or made available on the market or put into service.
- It must be drawn up in electronic format and, where requested, it must be provided in paper format.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The document that declares compliance with the EU requirements and assumes responsibility should be uploaded into the battery passport.

The ID of the EU declaration of conformity should also be displayed in addition to the document.

The CE marking pictogram should be included in the battery passport.

label: Compliance Test Report Results
sourceAttributeName: Results of test reports proving compliance
definition: A URI pointing to the results or summary of test reports carried out to demonstrate regulatory compliance.
shortDefinition: A URI pointing to the results or summary of test reports carried out to demonstrate regulatory compliance.
sourceShortDefinition: Test report results proving compliance with the requirements stated in the battery regulation
sourceCategory: Symbols, labels and documentation of conformity
accessRights: Notified bodies, market surveillance authorities and the Commission
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement: A battery passport must include the test report results that can prove the compliance with the requirements stated in the battery regulation.
dinSpecRecommendation: A placeholder for the reference to the test reports should be included as the format or specific contents of the test reports are not further specified yet.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (3); Annex VIII Part A 2(h)

label: Carbon Footprint per Functional Unit
sourceAttributeName: Battery carbon footprint per Functional Unit
definition: Total lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions attributable to the battery, expressed per kilowatt-hour of rated energy.
shortDefinition: Total lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions attributable to the battery, expressed per kilowatt-hour of rated energy.
sourceShortDefinition: The battery carbon footprint must be included as declared in terms of kg of carbon dioxide equivalent per one kWh of the total energy provided by the battery over its expected service life.
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must contain carbon footprint per functional unit of the battery as declared in the battery carbon footprint declaration in accordance with the entry into force of the implementing acts on the format of declaration.
dinSpecRecommendation: The carbon footprint of the battery shall also be differentiated as contribution of the lifecycle stages to the battery carbon footprint (see separate data attributes)
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (1c) → Art. 7; EV CF DA (draft); CF declaration IA (draft)

label: Raw Materials Lifecycle Carbon Contribution
sourceAttributeName: Contribution of raw material acquisition and pre-processing lifecycle stage
definition: The share of the battery's total carbon footprint attributable to the extraction and preprocessing of raw materials.
shortDefinition: The share of the battery's total carbon footprint attributable to the extraction and preprocessing of raw materials.
sourceShortDefinition: Contribution of raw material acquisition and pre-processing lifecycle stage to the battery carbon footprint
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include the contribution of the lifecycle stage “Raw material acquisition and pre-processing” in the battery carbon footprint.

The system boundaries and reporting requirements are specified in the delegated acts for the carbon footprint methodology outlined in "Battery carbon footprint per Functional Unit"

The same mandatory requirements as for "Battery carbon footprint per Functional Unit" are required for this data point.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (1c) → Art. 7; Annex II (4); EV CF DA (draft); CF declaration IA (draft)

label: Production Lifecycle Carbon Contribution
sourceAttributeName: Contribution of main product production lifecycle stage
definition: The portion of the battery's carbon footprint arising from the main manufacturing and assembly processes.
shortDefinition: The portion of the battery's carbon footprint arising from the main manufacturing and assembly processes.
sourceShortDefinition: Contribution of the main product production lifecycle stage to the battery carbon footprint
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include the contribution of the lifecycle stage “Main product production” in the battery carbon footprint.

The system boundaries and reporting requirements are specified in the delegated acts for the carbon footprint methodology outlined in "Battery carbon footprint per Functional Unit"

The same mandatory requirements as for "Battery carbon footprint per Functional Unit" are required for this data point.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (1c) → Art. 7; Annex II (4); EV CF DA (draft); CF declaration IA (draft)

label: Distribution Lifecycle Carbon Contribution
definition: The carbon emissions associated with transporting and distributing the battery from manufacturing site to market.
shortDefinition: The carbon emissions associated with transporting and distributing the battery from manufacturing site to market.
sourceShortDefinition: Contribution of the distribution lifecycle stage to the battery's carbon footprint
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include the contribution of the lifecycle stage “Distribution” in the battery carbon footprint.

The system boundaries and reporting requirements are specified in the delegated acts for the carbon footprint methodology outlined in "Battery carbon footprint per Functional Unit"

The same mandatory requirements as for "Battery carbon footprint per Functional Unit" are required for this data point.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII 1(c) → Art. 7; Annex II (4); EV CF DA (draft); CF declaration IA (draft)

label: End-of-life Lifecycle Carbon Contribution
sourceAttributeName: Contribution of end of life and recycling lifecycle stage
definition: The carbon footprint contribution from end-of-life treatment and recycling of the battery and its components.
shortDefinition: The carbon footprint contribution from end-of-life treatment and recycling of the battery and its components.
sourceShortDefinition: Contribution of the end of life and recycling lifecycle stage to the battery's carbon footprint
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include the contribution of the lifecycle stage “End of life and Recycling” in the battery carbon footprint.

The system boundaries and reporting requirements are specified in the delegated acts for the carbon footprint methodology outlined in "Battery carbon footprint per Functional Unit"

The same mandatory requirements as for "Battery carbon footprint per Functional Unit" are required for this data point.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (1c) → Art. 7; Annex II (4); EV CF DA (draft); CF declaration IA (draft)

label: Carbon Footprint Performance Class
definition: A letter grade or class designation ranking the battery's carbon intensity relative to regulatory thresholds.
shortDefinition: A letter grade or class designation ranking the battery's carbon intensity relative to regulatory thresholds.
sourceShortDefinition: Depending on the distribution of the values in the carbon footprint declarations of batteries placed on the market, a meaningful number of classes of performance shall be identified, with category A being the best class with the lowest carbon footprint life cycle impact, to enable market differentiation
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must include the carbon footprint performance class in accordance with the entry into force of the delegated and implementing acts on the carbon footprint performance classes per Article 7 of the Battery Regulation.

The carbon footprint performance class shall be indicated on the carbon footprint label.
dinSpecRecommendation: The carbon footprint performance class shall be provided on a conspicuous, clearly legible and indelible label (see 6.2.4 "Carbon footprint label").
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (1c); Art. 7(2); Labelling IA (draft) Annex V

label: Public Carbon Footprint Study URL
sourceAttributeName: Web link to public carbon footprint study
definition: A publicly accessible URI linking to the full lifecycle carbon footprint study underpinning the reported values.
shortDefinition: A publicly accessible URI linking to the full lifecycle carbon footprint study underpinning the reported values.
sourceShortDefinition: The public version of the carbon footprint study
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include a web link to the carbon footprint study accompanying the battery carbon footprint declaration. The information and reporting requirements are specified in the corresponding delegated acts as outlined in 6.3.2 "Battery carbon footprint per Functional Unit".
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (1c); Art. 7 1(g); EV CF DA (draft)

label: Absolute Carbon Footprint
definition: The total lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions of the battery expressed as an absolute value in kilograms of CO2 equivalent.
shortDefinition: The total lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions of the battery expressed as an absolute value in kilograms of CO2 equivalent.
sourceShortDefinition: Battery carbon footprint as absolute number
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
dinSpecRecommendation:
As a non-mandatory data attribute, the battery passport should include the battery carbon footprint in absolute terms.

The absolute battery carbon footprint should be calculated as kilograms of carbon dioxide equivalent, without reference to the functional unit as prescribed by the battery regulation.

label: Due Diligence Report
definition: A URI linking to the published supply chain due diligence report covering responsible sourcing of battery materials.
shortDefinition: A URI linking to the published supply chain due diligence report covering responsible sourcing of battery materials.
sourceShortDefinition: The report on the supply chain due diligence policy, risk management plan, and summary of third-party verification.
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must include the “information on responsible sourcing as indicated in the report on its due diligence policy referred to in BattReg Article 52(3)”.

The due diligence report must be accessible free of charge to the public via the battery passport and also on the internet.

The report and hence also the information in the battery passport must provide data and information on four aspects:
- Supply chain due diligence policy: steps taken by the economic operator to comply with the requirements of Article 49.
- Risk management plan: steps taken by the economic operator to comply with the requirements of Article 50, including findings of significant adverse impacts.
- Summary report of the third-party verification carried out (Article 51).
- An elaboration on access to information, public participation in decision-making and access to justice.
dinSpecRecommendation:
Only the current annual due diligence report valid for the specific battery at the time of placing it on the market should be linked.

The due diligence reports should be made available as PDFs.

For the battery passport, a link to the PDF uploaded to the company website should be made available.

label: Third-party Scheme Assurances
sourceAttributeName: Third party assurances of recognised schemes
definition: Declarations or certifications from independent third-party organisations confirming adherence to recognised responsible sourcing schemes.
shortDefinition: Declarations or certifications from independent third-party organisations confirming adherence to recognised responsible sourcing schemes.
sourceShortDefinition: Information on supply chain scheme assurances granted by third-party actors
dinSpecRecommendation: As a non-mandatory data attribute the battery passport should contain information on third-party supply chain assurances such as certifications, of recognised supply chain schemes. Based on the provided guidance, explore which and how to best make third-party assurances available via the battery passport

label: Supply Chain Indices
definition: Quantitative or categorical indicators measuring the social and environmental performance of the battery supply chain.
shortDefinition: Quantitative or categorical indicators measuring the social and environmental performance of the battery supply chain.
sourceShortDefinition: Information on relevant supply chain indices
dinSpecRecommendation: As a non-mandatory data attribute the battery passport should contain supply chain indices to allow easy assessment for consumers on level of responsible sourcing. ESGE+ indices under development by the GBA (Global Battery Alliance), scoring and benchmarking sustainability performance. Once developed, explore how to best make the indices available via the battery passport.

label: Battery Chemistry
definition: A description of the electrochemical technology used in the battery, such as lithium-ion, nickel-metal hydride, or lead-acid.
shortDefinition: A description of the electrochemical technology used in the battery, such as lithium-ion, nickel-metal hydride, or lead-acid.
sourceShortDefinition: The electrochemical composition reflecting active materials in cathode and anode, and - voluntarily - electrolyte
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must contain information on the battery chemistry of the battery.

The battery chemistry must also be provided on the battery label

Base on the labelling IA (currently available as draft), the following terms shall be used:

Lead acid: “Pb”
Nickel cadmium: “Ni-Cd”
Nickel metal hydride: “Ni-MH”
Lithium ion NMC: “Li-ion NMC”
Lithium ion LFP: “Li-ion LFP”
Lithium ion LMO: “Li-ion LMO”
Lithium ion LCO: “Li-ion LCO”
Lithium ion NCA: “Li-ion NCA”
Lithium metal: “Li-metal”
Sodium-ion: “Na-ion”

For secondary (rechargeable) batteries whose electrochemical composition does not correspond to any of the categories listed above, it shall be indicated based on the molecular formula of the cathode and/or anode.
dinSpecRecommendation: The battery chemistry should relate to the naming of the cathode, anode, and electrolyte of the battery.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (1b); Annex VI Part A (7); Labelling IA (draft)

label: Critical Raw Materials
definition: A list of materials classified as critical raw materials that are present in the battery above defined threshold concentrations.
shortDefinition: A list of materials classified as critical raw materials that are present in the battery above defined threshold concentrations.
sourceShortDefinition: Critical raw materials present in a concentration of more than 0,1%, weight by weight
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must contain information on the critical raw materials present in the battery. Critical raw materials are listed in Annex II of Regulation (EU) 2024/1252.

The information on the critical raw materials must also be provided on the battery label.

Per Annex VI, Part A(10), critical raw materials must be reported if present in the battery in a concentration of more than 0,1 % weight by weight.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (1b); Annex VI Part A (10); Labelling IA (draft)

label: Cathode, Anode and Electrolyte Materials
sourceAttributeName: Materials used in cathode, anode and electrolyte
definition: Lists of the active materials and chemical compounds used in the cathode, anode, and electrolyte of the battery.
shortDefinition: Lists of the active materials and chemical compounds used in the cathode, anode, and electrolyte of the battery.
sourceShortDefinition: Detailed composition of cathode, anode and electrolyte
accessRights: persons with a legitimate interest and the Commission
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must contain information on the detailed composition of the battery.

Information on the detailed composition must include materials used in the cathode, anode and electrolyte.
dinSpecRecommendation:
Access to this information should be additionally, at least, provided to sorters, dismantlers, and recyclers.

A "material" should relate to the composition of its constituent chemical components in order to report on the detailed composition of a battery model by specifying the materials used in the entire battery.

Materials should be reported for the as-built battery.

Materials in the battery should be reported above a concentration of 1 % weight by weight. Thereby, the threshold of 1 % should be determined as the overall content of the material in the battery in relation to the mass of the entire battery, as defined per technical documentation referred to in Annex VIII.

The mass should be specified in terms of kg of a material in the entire battery, as defined per technical documentation referred to in BattReg Annex VIII.

The materials should be named using a public standard or agreed nomenclature, which refers e.g. to the nomenclature of IUPAC or related identifiers such as the CAS (Chemical Abstracts Service) number.

label: Hazardous Substances
definition: Identification of hazardous chemical substances present in the battery that require disclosure under applicable regulations.
shortDefinition: Identification of hazardous chemical substances present in the battery that require disclosure under applicable regulations.
sourceShortDefinition: Substances of very high concern in the meaning of Article 3 (1) (69) which are present in the battery, other than mercury, cadmium and lead, with a concentration equal or above 0,1%, weight on weight.’
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must contain information on substances of very high concern other than mercury, cadmium, or lead contained in the battery.

substances of very high concern in the meaning of Article 3 (1) (69) which are present in the battery, other than mercury, cadmium and lead, with a concentration equal or above 0,1%, weight on weight.
‘substance of very high concern’ means any substance which fulfils the criteria laid down in Article 57 of Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 [REACH] and is identified in accordance with Article 59(1) of that Regulation, or any substance which fulfils the criteria laid down in Article 57 of Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 [REACH] and listed in Annex VI of Regulation (EC) 1272/2008 [CLP Regulation].’

Substances of very high concern and those for which there is a restriction in BattReg Annex I [mercury, cadmium, lead] are required to be named in accordance with their nomenclature in Regulation (EC) 1272/2008 ("CLP Regulation").

The information on hazardous substances must also be provided on the battery label.

BattReg Article 74(3) states that the location of the hazardous substances in the battery must be identified. This requirement needs to be provided upon request even though it is not specifically for the battery passport and can be integrated.
dinSpecRecommendation:
Substances should be reported on as chemical element and its compounds in the natural state or obtained by any manufacturing process, as per Regulations EC No 1907/2006 and EC No 1272/2008.

A nomenclature for hazardous substances should relate to the nomenclature of IUPAC, as required under Regulation EC No 1907/2006. Other identifiers such as the CAS (Chemical Abstracts Service) or EC number can be added if available.

The hazards of the substances should be specified.(see separate data attribute)

The mass of the hazardous substances should be reported for a battery model.
The mass can be reported in terms of kg of a material and in terms of the concentration of weight by weight % in the entire battery, as defined per technical documentation referred to in Annex VIII. The SCIP guidance for concentration ranges should be used.
sourceRegulationReference:
Annex XIII (1b); Annex VI Part A (8); Labelling IA (draft),
Environmental Omnibus: Draft: 2025/0397 (COD), Art. 1 (4)

label: Substance Environmental and Health Impact
sourceAttributeName: Impact of substances on environment, human health, safety, persons
definition: Information about the potential environmental and human health effects associated with substances present in the battery.
shortDefinition: Information about the potential environmental and human health effects associated with substances present in the battery.
sourceShortDefinition: Elaboration on the impact of substances contained in the battery on the environment, human health and the safety of persons
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must contain information on the impact of substances, in particular hazardous substances, present in the battery. Hereby the impact on the environment and on human health or the safety of persons is considered and includes the impact due to inappropriate discarding of waste batteries, such as littering or discarding as unsorted municipal waste.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The classification for “hazardous substances” should be defined as per Regulation EC No 1272/2008 (“CLP Regulation”).

The impact should be described by using commonly used statements, which can be reported by hazard (H) phrases and precautionary (P) statements as per Regulation EC No 1272/2008 (“CLP Regulation”).

The impact should be reported as required per Regulation EC No 1907/2006 (“REACH Regulation”) registration obligations.

The impact of substances on environment, human health, safety, persons may also be included in the data attribute 'hazardous substances'

label: Dismantling and Removal Manual
sourceAttributeName: Dismantling information: Manuals for the removal and the disassembly of the battery pack
definition: A URI referencing the technical manual detailing safe procedures for dismantling and removing the battery from its application.
shortDefinition: A URI referencing the technical manual detailing safe procedures for dismantling and removing the battery from its application.
sourceShortDefinition: Manual(s) containing information on the removal and disassembly of the battery pack
accessRights: persons with a legitimate interest and the Commission

label: Component Part Numbers
definition: A URI or reference document listing the manufacturer part numbers for all serviceable or replaceable battery components.
shortDefinition: A URI or reference document listing the manufacturer part numbers for all serviceable or replaceable battery components.
sourceShortDefinition: The part numbers of components of the battery
accessRights: persons with a legitimate interest and the Commission
regulatoryRequirement: A battery passport must include part numbers for components.

label: Spare Parts Source Information
sourceAttributeName: Information on sources of spare parts
definition: Details about where and how to obtain genuine spare parts or components for battery maintenance and repair.
shortDefinition: Details about where and how to obtain genuine spare parts or components for battery maintenance and repair.
sourceShortDefinition: Information on how to contact sources of spare parts
accessRights: persons with a legitimate interest and the Commission
regulatoryRequirement: A battery passport must include contact details of sources for spare parts (replacement spares).
dinSpecRecommendation:
As part of their contact details, economic operators should indicate a postal and, if available, email and website address.

The postal address, e-mail address and web address of the sources for spare parts should be provided together with the part numbers of components.

The information on spare parts should be provided within the two manuals in 6.6.1.2.

label: Safety Measures
definition: Precautions and protective measures that must be observed when handling, storing, or working on the battery.
shortDefinition: Precautions and protective measures that must be observed when handling, storing, or working on the battery.
sourceShortDefinition: Safety measures to be taken during battery handling, including the dismantling
accessRights: persons with a legitimate interest and the Commission
regulatoryRequirement: A battery passport must include safety measures.
dinSpecRecommendation: The safety measures should be provided via the instruction manuals as URL linking to PDF.

label: Pre-consumer Recycled Nickel Share
definition: The percentage of nickel in the battery that originates from pre-consumer recycled sources such as manufacturing scrap.
shortDefinition: The percentage of nickel in the battery that originates from pre-consumer recycled sources such as manufacturing scrap.
sourceShortDefinition: The share of nickel recycled from pre-consumer waste in the active materials of the battery
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include recycled content information.

The content information must include the percentage share of nickel that is present in active materials and that has been recovered from battery manufacturing waste, for each battery model per year and per manufacturing plant.

Pre-consumer waste or “battery manufacturing waste” is “the materials or objects rejected during the battery manufacturing process, which cannot be re-used as an integral part in the same process and need to be recycled”
dinSpecRecommendation: The recycled content shares from pre-consumer waste and post-consumer waste of cobalt, lithium, nickel in active materials and lead present in the battery should be calculated and declared separately.

label: Pre-consumer Recycled Cobalt Share
definition: The percentage of cobalt in the battery derived from pre-consumer recycled material streams.
shortDefinition: The percentage of cobalt in the battery derived from pre-consumer recycled material streams.
sourceShortDefinition: The share of cobalt recycled from pre-consumer waste in the active materials of the battery
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include recycled content information.

The content information must include the percentage share of cobalt that is present in active materials and that has been recovered from battery manufacturing waste, for each battery model per year and per manufacturing plant.

Pre-consumer waste or “battery manufacturing waste” is “the materials or objects rejected during the battery manufacturing process, which cannot be re-used as an integral part in the same process and need to be recycled”
dinSpecRecommendation: The recycled content shares from pre-consumer waste and post-consumer waste of cobalt, lithium, nickel in active materials and lead present in the battery should be calculated and declared separately.

label: Pre-consumer Recycled Lithium Share
definition: The proportion of lithium content in the battery originating from pre-consumer recycling processes.
shortDefinition: The proportion of lithium content in the battery originating from pre-consumer recycling processes.
sourceShortDefinition: The share of lithium recycled from pre-consumer waste in the active materials of the battery
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include recycled content information.

The content information must include the percentage share of lithium that is present in active materials and that has been recovered from battery manufacturing waste, for each battery model per year and per manufacturing plant.

Pre-consumer waste or “battery manufacturing waste” is “the materials or objects rejected during the battery manufacturing process, which cannot be re-used as an integral part in the same process and need to be recycled”
dinSpecRecommendation: The recycled content shares from pre-consumer waste and post-consumer waste of cobalt, lithium, nickel in active materials and lead present in the battery should be calculated and declared separately.

label: Post-consumer Recycled Nickel Share
definition: The percentage of nickel in the battery sourced from post-consumer recycled material collected after end-user use.
shortDefinition: The percentage of nickel in the battery sourced from post-consumer recycled material collected after end-user use.
sourceShortDefinition: The share of nickel recycled from post-consumer waste in the active materials of the battery
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include recycled content information.

The content information must include the percentage share of nickel that is present in active materials and that has been recovered from post-consumer waste, for each battery model per year and per manufacturing plant.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The recycled content shares from pre-consumer waste and post-consumer waste of cobalt, lithium, nickel in active materials and lead present in the battery should be calculated and declared separately.

Post-consumer material is defined in DIN EN ISO 14021 as the “material generated by households or by commercial, industrial, and institutional facilities in their role as end-users of the product, which can no longer be used for its intended purpose. This includes returns of material from the distribution chain.”

label: Post-consumer Recycled Cobalt Share
definition: The proportion of cobalt in the battery that comes from post-consumer recycled material streams.
shortDefinition: The proportion of cobalt in the battery that comes from post-consumer recycled material streams.
sourceShortDefinition: The share of cobalt recycled from post-consumer waste in the active materials of the battery
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include recycled content information.

The content information must include the percentage share of cobalt that is present in active materials and that has been recovered from post-consumer waste, for each battery model per year and per manufacturing plant.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The recycled content shares from pre-consumer waste and post-consumer waste of cobalt, lithium, nickel in active materials and lead present in the battery should be calculated and declared separately.

Post-consumer material is defined in DIN EN ISO 14021 as the “material generated by households or by commercial, industrial, and institutional facilities in their role as end-users of the product, which can no longer be used for its intended purpose. This includes returns of material from the distribution chain.”

label: Post-consumer Recycled Lithium Share
definition: The share of lithium content in the battery derived from post-consumer recycled sources.
shortDefinition: The share of lithium content in the battery derived from post-consumer recycled sources.
sourceShortDefinition: The share of lithium recycled from post-consumer waste in the active materials of the battery
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include recycled content information.

The content information must include the percentage share of lithium that is present in active materials and that has been recovered from post-consumer waste, for each battery model per year and per manufacturing plant.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The recycled content shares from pre-consumer waste and post-consumer waste of cobalt, lithium, nickel in active materials and lead present in the battery should be calculated and declared separately.

Post-consumer material is defined in DIN EN ISO 14021 as the “material generated by households or by commercial, industrial, and institutional facilities in their role as end-users of the product, which can no longer be used for its intended purpose. This includes returns of material from the distribution chain.”

label: Recycled Lead Share
definition: The percentage of lead used in the battery that has been recovered from recycled material sources.
shortDefinition: The percentage of lead used in the battery that has been recovered from recycled material sources.
sourceShortDefinition: The share of recycled lead in the active materials of the battery
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include recycled content information.

The content information must include the percentage share of lead that is present in the active materials of the battery and that has been recovered from waste, for each battery model per year and per manufacturing plant.

The regulation does not distinguish between pre- and post-consumer waste for the lead share.
dinSpecRecommendation: The recycled content share of lead present in the battery should be calculated and declared.

label: Renewable Content Share
definition: The percentage of battery material content derived from renewable biological or sustainable resource streams.
shortDefinition: The percentage of battery material content derived from renewable biological or sustainable resource streams.
sourceShortDefinition: Share of renewable content in the battery
granularityLevel: Battery model per calendar year and per manufacturing site, i.e. batch level
regulatoryRequirement: A battery passport must include information about the share of renewable content.
dinSpecRecommendation:
If the proportion of renewable content is greater than zero, specifications about which material is involved and its content shall be determined.

The renewable content share should be calculated as the mass of renewable material divided by the overall mass of the battery, using the formula: RM/(RM + NM)

Where RM = Mass of renewable materials
NM = Mass of non-renewable materials

Renewable material is defined as materials that are composed of biomass and that can be continually replenished, in EN 16575:2014.

label: End-user Waste Prevention Information
sourceAttributeName: Information on the role of end-users in contributing to waste prevention
definition: A URI pointing to guidance for end-users on how to extend battery life and avoid unnecessary waste.
shortDefinition: A URI pointing to guidance for end-users on how to extend battery life and avoid unnecessary waste.
sourceShortDefinition: Information on how end-users can contribute to waste prevention
sourceSubcategory: Information on role of end-users in waste prevention and information on battery collection, preparation for second life and on treatment at end of life
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include information on the role of end-users in contributing to waste prevention.

The information must include good practices and recommendations concerning the use of batteries aimed at extending their use phase and the possibilities of re-use, preparation for re-use, preparation for repurposing, repurposing and remanufacturing.
dinSpecRecommendation: General information on the role of the end-users in waste prevention and information on good practices and recommendations concerning the use of batteries aiming at extending their use-phase should be provided as link to PDF.

label: Separate Waste Collection Information
sourceAttributeName: Information on the role of end-users in contributing to the separate collection of waste batteries
definition: A URI linking to information for end-users about how and where to deposit spent batteries for separate collection.
shortDefinition: A URI linking to information for end-users about how and where to deposit spent batteries for separate collection.
sourceShortDefinition: Information on how end-users can contribute to the separate collection of waste batteries
sourceSubcategory: Information on role of end-users in waste prevention and information on battery collection, preparation for second life and on treatment at end of life
regulatoryRequirement:
A battery passport must include information on the role of end-users in contributing to the separate collection of waste batteries.

End-users are obliged to discard waste batteries separately from other waste streams, including from mixed municipal waste and in designated separate collection points set up by, or in accordance with the specific arrangements concluded with, the producer or a producer responsibility organisation.
dinSpecRecommendation:
Information on the practical role of the end-users in contributing to the separate collection of waste batteries should be provided as link to PDF.

The European Commission guidelines on the facilitation of the harmonised application of provisions on the removability and replaceability of LMT batteries state that the information on waste prevention provided to the end-user should include clear advice on the next steps required to ensure that waste batteries are not discarded with other waste. Information should also be provided on the correct handling, packaging, storage and transport of the waste battery to a separate collection point or treatment facility.

label: End-of-life and Collection Information
sourceAttributeName: Information on battery collection, preparation for second life and on treatment at end of life
definition: A URI referencing comprehensive guidance on battery collection, preparation for second-life use, and proper end-of-life treatment.
shortDefinition: A URI referencing comprehensive guidance on battery collection, preparation for second-life use, and proper end-of-life treatment.
sourceShortDefinition: Information on the collection of waste batteries, their preparation for second life and definitive treatment at end of life
sourceSubcategory: Information on role of end-users in waste prevention and information on battery collection, preparation for second life and on treatment at end of life
regulatoryRequirement: A battery passport must include information on the separate collection, take-back and collection points, preparation for re-use, preparation for repurposing and treatment available for waste batteries.
dinSpecRecommendation: The location of collection points, a description of the take-back process, preparation for re-use, preparation for repurposing and treatment available should be provided as link to PDF.

label: Rated Capacity
definition: The total electrical charge the battery can deliver under specified conditions as declared by the manufacturer, expressed in ampere-hours.
shortDefinition: The total electrical charge the battery can deliver under specified conditions as declared by the manufacturer, expressed in ampere-hours.
sourceShortDefinition: The total number of ampere-hours that can be withdrawn from a fully charged battery under reference conditions
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must include information about the rated capacity.

The rated capacity of LMT batteries shall be determined according to reliable, accurate and reproducible methods which take into account the generally recognised state-of-the-art methods, and the results of which are deemed to be of low uncertainty, including methods set out in standards for which references have been published for those purposes in the Official Journal of the European Union (OJEU).

Note: (For LMT batteries) the rated capacity is included in the required information for the battery label.
dinSpecRecommendation: The test method and reference conditions for measurement should include, among others, the battery charge and discharge rate, the resting period between charge and discharge as well as the operating temperature.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex IV Part A (1); Annex XIII (1g); Labelling IA (draft) Annex II Part A (VI) and Part C (C, 1)
regulationReferences: Labelling IA (draft) Annex II Part A (VI) and Part C (C, 1)

label: Remaining Capacity
definition: The charge capacity the battery can still deliver at the time of measurement, expressed in ampere-hours.
shortDefinition: The charge capacity the battery can still deliver at the time of measurement, expressed in ampere-hours.
sourceShortDefinition: The corresponding in-use data attribute to rated capacity
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must include information about the rated capacity.

The rated capacity of LMT batteries shall be determined according to reliable, accurate and reproducible methods which take into account the generally recognised state-of-the-art methods, and the results of which are deemed to be of low uncertainty, including methods set out in standards for which references have been published for those purposes in the Official Journal of the European Union (OJEU).

Note: (For LMT batteries) the rated capacity is included in the required information for the battery label.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The definition of remaining capacity shall consider the definition of "rated capacity" (6.7.2.2) to ensure comparability.

The test method and reference conditions for measurement should include, among others, the battery charge and discharge rate in terms of rated capacity (C-rate), the resting period in between charge and discharge as well as the operating temperature.

label: Capacity Fade
definition: The percentage reduction in the battery's charge capacity relative to its original rated capacity.
shortDefinition: The percentage reduction in the battery's charge capacity relative to its original rated capacity.
sourceShortDefinition: The decrease over time and upon usage in the amount of charge that a battery can deliver at the rated voltage, with respect to the original rated capacity as declared by the manufacturer
updateRequirement: upon placement on market and battery status change
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must include information on the capacity fade.

The capacity fade must be updated upon change of the battery status.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex IV Part A (1); Annex IV (2)

label: Certified Usable Battery Energy
definition: The minimum usable energy the battery is certified to store and deliver, expressed in watt-hours.
shortDefinition: The minimum usable energy the battery is certified to store and deliver, expressed in watt-hours.
sourceShortDefinition: The usable battery energy according to the procedure in the UN GTR No 22 as determined during the certification of the vehicle
dinSpecRecommendation: The battery passport should include information on the remaining usable battery energy for EV batteries as it is useful in context of SOCE and needed for its calculation.

label: Remaining Usable Battery Energy
definition: The amount of usable energy the battery can still deliver at the point of measurement, expressed in watt-hours.
shortDefinition: The amount of usable energy the battery can still deliver at the point of measurement, expressed in watt-hours.
sourceShortDefinition: The usable battery energy at the present point in the lifetime of a battery as determined according to the procedure in the UN GTR No 22
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
dinSpecRecommendation: The update interval and its definition should be aligned with the update interval of the SOCE.

label: State of Certified Energy (SoCE)
definition: The ratio of remaining usable energy to the certified usable energy, expressed as a percentage.
shortDefinition: The ratio of remaining usable energy to the certified usable energy, expressed as a percentage.
sourceShortDefinition: The measured or on-board usable battery energy performance at a specific point in its lifetime, expressed as a percentage of the certified usable battery energy.
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information on the SOCE as information on the state of health of the battery pursuant to BattReg Article 14 for individual batteries.
dinSpecRecommendation:
SOCE shall be expressed as a percentage of the certified usable battery energy.

The SOCE shall be reported based on the conditions laid out in the UN GTR No 22.

label: State of Charge (SoC)
definition: The current charge level of the battery expressed as a percentage of its full charge capacity.
shortDefinition: The current charge level of the battery expressed as a percentage of its full charge capacity.
sourceShortDefinition: The available energy or charge in a battery expressed as a percentage of rated capacity as declared by the manufacturer. When the battery’s state of health is no longer equal to its initial condition, SoC refers to the maximum energy or charge that can be stored in the battery at the time of charging.
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport shall include the state of charge (SoC) of the battery.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The following recommendation is made:
The information on the SoC should additionally be reported by use of a histogram that represents the time spent in specific SoC intervals covering the entire range of SoC values. The BMS should aggregate the time spent in each SoC interval from values during operation of the battery
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (4d); Article 3 1(27); Standardization request M/579

label: Minimum Voltage
definition: The lowest terminal voltage the battery may reach during discharge without causing damage or loss of function.
shortDefinition: The lowest terminal voltage the battery may reach during discharge without causing damage or loss of function.
sourceShortDefinition: The lower voltage limit that the safe operation of the battery is rated for
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information on the minimal voltage, with temperature ranges when relevant.
dinSpecRecommendation: The temperature range, in which the provided minimal voltage applies, should be reported in °C.

label: Maximum Voltage
definition: The highest terminal voltage permitted during charging without exceeding safe operating limits.
shortDefinition: The highest terminal voltage permitted during charging without exceeding safe operating limits.
sourceShortDefinition: The upper voltage limit that the safe operation of the battery is rated for
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information about the maximum voltage, with temperature ranges when relevant.
dinSpecRecommendation: The temperature range, in which the provided maximum voltage applies, should be reported in °C.

label: Nominal Voltage
definition: The representative voltage at which the battery is rated to operate under normal load conditions.
shortDefinition: The representative voltage at which the battery is rated to operate under normal load conditions.
sourceShortDefinition: The suitable approximate value of the voltage used to designate or identify the battery
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information about the nominal voltage, with temperature ranges when relevant.

label: Original Power Capability
definition: The maximum power output the battery was capable of delivering when new, expressed in watts.
shortDefinition: The maximum power output the battery was capable of delivering when new, expressed in watts.
sourceShortDefinition: The amount of energy that a battery is capable to provide over a given period of time under reference conditions
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information about the original power capability […], with temperature range when relevant.
dinSpecRecommendation:
Power capability shall be measured at reference conditions, which must include measurements at 80 % and 20 % state of charge for EV and industrial batteries.

The temperature range, in which the provided maximum voltage applies, should be provided in the unit °C.

The test method and reference conditions should, among others, include the charge and discharge rate in terms of rated capacity (C-rate), the duration and the voltage at the point of measurement.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (1i); Art. 10, Annex IV Part B (4) → measurement at 80 % SoC and 20% SoC required; Annex IV (3)
regulationReferences: Art. 10, Annex IV Part B (4) → measurement at 80 % SoC and 20% SoC required

label: Remaining Power Capability
definition: The current maximum power output the battery is able to deliver at the point of measurement, in watts.
shortDefinition: The current maximum power output the battery is able to deliver at the point of measurement, in watts.
sourceShortDefinition: The amount of energy that a battery is capable to provide over a given period of time under reference conditions at a distinct point in time during its usage
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must, where possible, contain information on the remaining power capability as information on the state of health of the battery
dinSpecRecommendation: Power capability shall be measured at reference conditions, which, among others, must include the measurement at 80 % and 20 % SoC.
sourceRegulationReference: Art. 10: Annex IV (3) (only definition of power); Annex VII Part A (2) "where possible, remaining power capability"; Annex IV Part B (4) --> measurement at 80 % SoC and 20% SoC required
regulationReferences: Art. 10: Annex IV (3) (only definition of power)
Annex VII Part A (2) "where possible, remaining power capability"
Annex IV Part B (4) --> measurement at 80 % SoC and 20% SoC required

label: Power Fade
definition: The reduction in maximum power capability since the battery left the factory, expressed in watts.
shortDefinition: The reduction in maximum power capability since the battery left the factory, expressed in watts.
sourceShortDefinition: The decrease over time and upon usage in the amount of power that a battery can deliver at the rated voltage
updateRequirement: upon placement on market and battery status change
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must contain information on the power fade.

The update interval must be upon placement on the market and upon change of the battery status.
sourceRegulationReference:
