Annex IV P(4) ("power fade" definition); Annex IV Part A (2) (power fade in %)
Annex IV Part B (4) → measurement at 80 % SoC required;
regulationReferences: Annex IV Part A (2) (power fade in %)
Annex IV Part B (4) → measurement at 80 % SoC required

label: Maximum Permitted Battery Power
definition: The upper limit of power the battery is permitted to deliver or absorb without risk of damage or unsafe operation.
shortDefinition: The upper limit of power the battery is permitted to deliver or absorb without risk of damage or unsafe operation.
sourceShortDefinition: The value of maximum permitted power the battery is rated for and reflects the data relevant for power limits
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information about the original power capability and limits, with temperature range when relevant.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The maximum permitted battery power should be defined as the upper power limit that the safe operation of the battery is rated for.

The temperature range, in which the provided maximum permitted battery power applies, should be provided in unit °C.

label: Power to Energy Ratio
sourceAttributeName: Ratio between nominal battery power and battery energy
definition: The ratio of the battery's nominal power to its nominal energy, indicating how power-dense or energy-dense the design is.
shortDefinition: The ratio of the battery's nominal power to its nominal energy, indicating how power-dense or energy-dense the design is.
sourceShortDefinition: The suitable approximate value of the power capability used to designate or identify the battery, while the battery energy is determined in reference conditions to be defined
updateRequirement: upon placement on market and battery status change
dinSpecRecommendation: As a non-mandatory data point the battery passport should include the ratio between nominal battery power in W and battery energy in Wh. It aims to provide an indication of the applicable discharge and charge rate in terms of rated capacity (C-rate) (see BattReg Annex IV Part B(2)).

label: Initial Round-trip Energy Efficiency
definition: The ratio of energy discharged to energy charged over a complete charge-discharge cycle when the battery was new, expressed as a percentage.
shortDefinition: The ratio of energy discharged to energy charged over a complete charge-discharge cycle when the battery was new, expressed as a percentage.
sourceShortDefinition: The ratio of the net energy delivered by a battery during a discharge test to the total energy required to restore the initial state of charge by a standard charge (see BattReg Annex IV (6))
sourceSubcategory: Round trip energy efficiency and self-discharge
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information about the initial round trip energy efficiency.
dinSpecRecommendation:
Initial round trip energy efficiency should be measured at reference conditions.

The initial state of charge, referred to in the definition above, should correspond to a fully charged state.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (1n); Art. 10: Annex IV (6); Annex IV Part A (4)

label: Round-trip Efficiency at 50% Cycle Life
sourceAttributeName: Round trip energy efficiency at 50% of cycle life
definition: The measured round-trip energy efficiency at the midpoint of the battery's expected charge-discharge cycle life.
shortDefinition: The measured round-trip energy efficiency at the midpoint of the battery's expected charge-discharge cycle life.
sourceShortDefinition: The ratio of the net energy delivered by a battery during a discharge test to the total energy required to restore the initial state of charge by a standard charge (see BattReg Annex IV (6))
sourceSubcategory: Round trip energy efficiency and self-discharge
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information about the round trip energy efficiency at 50 % of cycle-life.
dinSpecRecommendation:
Initial round trip energy efficiency shall be measured at reference conditions that include the specification of “50 % of cycle-life”.

Test methods and reference conditions should separately consider non-cycle applications and batteries with very long cycle-life.

label: Remaining Round-trip Energy Efficiency
definition: The current round-trip energy efficiency of the battery measured at the time of reporting.
shortDefinition: The current round-trip energy efficiency of the battery measured at the time of reporting.
sourceShortDefinition: The in-use analogy to the initial roundtrip energy the ratio of the net energy delivered by a battery during a discharge test to the total energy required to restore the initial state of charge by a standard charge
sourceSubcategory: Round trip energy efficiency and self-discharge
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must, where possible, include information about the remaining round-trip energy efficiency as information on the state of health of the battery.
dinSpecRecommendation: The update frequency of remaining round trip energy efficiency should be aligned with the update frequency of round trip energy efficiency fade and should be provided upon change of the battery status.
sourceRegulationReference: Art. 10: Annex IV Part A (4); Article 14: Annex VII Part A (3); Annex IV (6)

label: Energy Efficiency Fade
definition: The percentage decline in round-trip energy efficiency compared to the battery's initial efficiency when new.
shortDefinition: The percentage decline in round-trip energy efficiency compared to the battery's initial efficiency when new.
sourceShortDefinition: The decrease of round trip energy efficiency as percentage
sourceSubcategory: Round trip energy efficiency and self-discharge
updateRequirement: upon placement on market and battery status change
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must, where applicable, contain information on the energy round trip efficiency fade.

The energy round trip efficiency fade must be updated upon placement on the market and change of the battery status.

label: Initial Self-discharge Rate
definition: The rate at which the battery loses charge spontaneously when idle, measured when the battery was new, expressed as a percentage per unit time.
shortDefinition: The rate at which the battery loses charge spontaneously when idle, measured when the battery was new, expressed as a percentage per unit time.
sourceShortDefinition: The self-discharge-rate in an idle state of the battery in reference conditions (temperature etc.) at begin of life
sourceSubcategory: Round trip energy efficiency and self-discharge
dinSpecRecommendation:
The battery passport should contain information on the evolution of self-discharge rates for stationary BESS using a BMS and LMT batteries.

The information on initial self-discharge rate should be accessible for persons with a legitimate interest.

label: Current Self-discharge Rate
definition: The measured rate of spontaneous charge loss in the battery at the time of reporting.
shortDefinition: The measured rate of spontaneous charge loss in the battery at the time of reporting.
sourceShortDefinition: The change of the self-discharge rate in an idle state of the battery in reference conditions (temperature etc.) at aging parameter x, e.g. after a certain amount of storage time or, number of cycles
sourceSubcategory: Round trip energy efficiency and self-discharge
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
dinSpecRecommendation: The battery passport should contain information on the evolution of self-discharge rates for stationary BESS using a BMS and LMT batteries.

label: Self-discharge Rate Evolution
definition: A value or trend describing how the battery's self-discharge rate has changed over its operational lifetime.
shortDefinition: A value or trend describing how the battery's self-discharge rate has changed over its operational lifetime.
sourceShortDefinition: The change of self-discharge over time and usage, as percentage calculated from the initial and current self-discharge rate.
sourceSubcategory: Round trip energy efficiency and self-discharge
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must contain information on the evolution of self-discharge rates for stationary BESS and LMT batteries.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The evolution of self-discharging rates should be calculated from the initial and current self discharge rate.

The update interval for evolution of self-discharge rates should be upon placement on market and change of battery status.

label: Initial Internal Resistance
sourceAttributeName: Initial internal resistance of battery cell and pack (module recommended)
definition: The electrical resistance measured within the battery cell, module, or pack when first commissioned, expressed in ohms.
shortDefinition: The electrical resistance measured within the battery cell, module, or pack when first commissioned, expressed in ohms.
sourceShortDefinition: "Quotient of change of voltage of a battery by the corresponding change in discharge current under specified conditions” (IEV 482 03 36)
regulatoryRequirement:
BattReg Annex IV (5) states that ‘Internal resistance’ means the opposition to the flow of current within a cell or a battery under reference conditions, that is, the sum of electronic resistance and ionic resistance to the contribution to total effective resistance including inductive/capacitive properties. This definition differs from the definition according to the IEC Electropedia stated above. The battery passport must include information about the internal resistance.

The battery passport must include information about the internal resistance on both battery cell and pack level.
dinSpecRecommendation: The battery passport can include additional information about the initial internal resistance on battery module level, if available. It should be defined and calculated analogously as the requirements on battery pack level above.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (1o); Art. 10: Annex IV Part A (3) (+definition of internal resistance)
regulationReferences: Art. 10: Annex IV Part A (3) (+definition of internal resistance)

label: Internal Resistance Increase
sourceAttributeName: Internal resistance increase of pack (cell and module recommended)
definition: The percentage rise in battery internal resistance relative to its initial value, reflecting degradation over use.
shortDefinition: The percentage rise in battery internal resistance relative to its initial value, reflecting degradation over use.
sourceShortDefinition: The increase over time and upon usage of internal resistance
updateRequirement: upon placement on market and battery status change
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must include information about the internal resistance increase.

The internal resistance increase must be updated upon change of the battery status, which is associated with a new placement on market and described in 6.1.3.7 "battery status".
dinSpecRecommendation:
The internal resistance increase over time and upon usage should be defined based on the ratio of current and initial values as follows:
Rincrease(x) = (R(x)  - RBOL) / RBOL × 100 %
where
x 	is the aging parameter (e.g. storage time, number of cycles, etc.;);
R(x)	is the internal resistance at the aging parameter x; and
RBOL	is the internal resistance at begin of life.

The battery passport can include additional information about the internal resistance increase on battery cell and battery module level, if available. It should be defined and calculated analogously to the requirements on battery pack level above.

label: Expected Lifetime (years)
definition: The number of calendar years the battery is designed to remain in service under normal operating conditions.
shortDefinition: The number of calendar years the battery is designed to remain in service under normal operating conditions.
sourceShortDefinition: Expected life-time under the reference conditions for which they have been designed in terms of cycles, except for non-cycle applications, and calendar years
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must include information about the expected battery lifetime in calendar years.

The update interval must be upon placement on the market and upon change of the battery status.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex IV, Part A(5); Annex XIII (1); Annex XIII (4a)

label: Expected Lifetime (cycles)
sourceAttributeName: Expected lifetime: Number of charge-discharge cycles
definition: The number of full charge-discharge cycles the battery is designed to complete before reaching end-of-life criteria.
shortDefinition: The number of full charge-discharge cycles the battery is designed to complete before reaching end-of-life criteria.
sourceShortDefinition: Expected life-time under the reference conditions for which they have been designed in terms of cycles, except for non-cycle applications, and calendar years
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must include information about the expected battery lifetime expressed in cycles, and reference test used, except for non-cycle applications.

The reference test must consider reference conditions for which the battery has been designed.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The exception for non-cycle applications that is not included in the BattReg Annex XIII provision should apply to this data attribute in general.

Test methods and reference conditions are subject to the ongoing standardization process at CEN/CENELEC relating to standardization request M/579;
sourceRegulationReference: Annex IV, Part A(5); Annex XIII (1); Annex XIII (4a)

label: Full Charge-discharge Cycles
sourceAttributeName: Number of full charging and discharging cycles
definition: The total number of complete charge-discharge cycles the battery has undergone since first use.
shortDefinition: The total number of complete charge-discharge cycles the battery has undergone since first use.
sourceShortDefinition: In-use number of (full) charging and discharging cycles
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information on the full number of charging and discharging cycles.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The number of full charging and discharging cycles should be determined considering reference conditions.

The value for the number of full charging and discharging cycles should result from the aggregation of partial cycles occurring in the use phase.

The exception for non-cycle applications as mentioned in "Expected lifetime: Number of charge-discharge cycles" should apply to this data attribute as well.
sourceRegulationReference: Annex XIII (4d); Art. 14: Annex VII Part B (5)

label: Cycle-life Reference Test
definition: The name or designation of the standardised test procedure used to determine the battery's expected cycle life.
shortDefinition: The name or designation of the standardised test procedure used to determine the battery's expected cycle life.
sourceShortDefinition: The reference test for “Expected lifetime: Number of charge-discharge cycles”
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information about the expected battery lifetime expressed in cycles, and reference test used.
dinSpecRecommendation: The exception for non-cycle applications as mentioned in "Expected lifetime: Number of charge-discharge cycles" should apply to this data attribute as well.

label: Cycle-life C-rate
definition: The charge or discharge C-rate applied during the cycle-life reference test, expressed in amperes per ampere-hour.
shortDefinition: The charge or discharge C-rate applied during the cycle-life reference test, expressed in amperes per ampere-hour.
sourceShortDefinition: Measurement parameter for “Expected lifetime: Number of charge-discharge cycles”: Applied charge and discharge rate in terms of rated capacity (C-rate) of relevant cycle-life reference test
regulatoryRequirement:
The battery passport must include information about the C-rate of relevant cycle-life test.

The expected life-time of the battery under the reference conditions for which it has been designed must be documented in terms of cycles, except for non-cycle applications.
dinSpecRecommendation:
The C-rate should be provided separately for both the charge and discharge of the battery, if applicable.

The exception for non-cycle applications as mentioned in "Expected lifetime: Number of charge-discharge cycles" should apply to this data attribute as well.

label: Energy Throughput
definition: The cumulative amount of energy delivered by the battery over its entire operational life, expressed in watt-hours.
shortDefinition: The cumulative amount of energy delivered by the battery over its entire operational life, expressed in watt-hours.
sourceShortDefinition: The overall sum of the energy throughput over the battery lifetime at a specific time during usage
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must contain information on the energy throughput over the battery lifetime.
dinSpecRecommendation: The data attribute should be reported as measured for further potential processing. In addition, the normalisation by capacity may add a further useful value that ensures comparability among battery sizes.

label: Capacity Throughput
definition: The total electrical charge the battery has delivered throughout its life, expressed in ampere-hours.
shortDefinition: The total electrical charge the battery has delivered throughout its life, expressed in ampere-hours.
sourceShortDefinition: The overall sum of the capacity throughput over the battery lifetime at a specific time during usage
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must contain information on the capacity throughput.
dinSpecRecommendation: The data attribute should be reported as measured for further potential processing. In addition, the normalisation by capacity may add a further useful value that ensures comparability among battery sizes.

label: Capacity Threshold for Exhaustion
definition: The minimum percentage of rated capacity below which the battery is considered exhausted or unsuitable for its intended application.
shortDefinition: The minimum percentage of rated capacity below which the battery is considered exhausted or unsuitable for its intended application.
sourceShortDefinition: The percentage of SOCE, above which the battery is still considered operational as EV battery in its current life, as provided by the economic operator
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information about the capacity threshold for exhaustion.

label: Temperature Information
definition: General temperature-related information relevant to the battery's safe and optimal operation.
shortDefinition: General temperature-related information relevant to the battery's safe and optimal operation.
sourceShortDefinition: Temperature information regarding operating environmental conditions
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include periodically recorded information on the operating environmental conditions, including temperature.
dinSpecRecommendation:
Additionally, histograms should be used for the time spent charging in extreme temperatures, to keep the implementation effort and storage size lean. That means dividing the entire range of temperature values into a series of intervals, including above and below threshold of extreme temperatures, and then accumulate the time spent in each interval.

Any temperature measurement on an in-use battery should be defined in more detail. Specifically, the location of temperature sensors will decide upon the merit of values provided in temperature data attributes and their applicability to the entire battery, which may encounter temperature gradients.

label: Temperature Range Lower Boundary
sourceAttributeName: Temperature range idle state, lower boundary
definition: The minimum ambient temperature at which the battery can be stored or operated without adverse effects.
shortDefinition: The minimum ambient temperature at which the battery can be stored or operated without adverse effects.
sourceShortDefinition: The lower boundary of the surrounding temperature range
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information about the temperature range the battery can withstand when not in use and a reference test must be provided.

label: Temperature Range Upper Boundary
sourceAttributeName: Temperature range idle state, upper boundary
definition: The maximum ambient temperature at which the battery can be stored or operated without risk of damage.
shortDefinition: The maximum ambient temperature at which the battery can be stored or operated without risk of damage.
sourceShortDefinition: The upper boundary of the surrounding temperature range
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information about the temperature range the battery can withstand when not in use and a reference test must be provided.

label: Time in High Temperature
sourceAttributeName: Time spent in extreme temperatures above boundary
definition: The cumulative duration in seconds the battery has been exposed to temperatures above the defined upper boundary.
shortDefinition: The cumulative duration in seconds the battery has been exposed to temperatures above the defined upper boundary.
sourceShortDefinition: The aggregated time, in which temperatures above the upper boundary of the temperature range as defined in 'Temperature range idle state, upper boundary' are prevalent
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information on the time spent in extreme temperatures

label: Time in Low Temperature
sourceAttributeName: Time spent in extreme temperatures below boundary
definition: The total time in seconds the battery has been exposed to temperatures below the defined lower boundary.
shortDefinition: The total time in seconds the battery has been exposed to temperatures below the defined lower boundary.
sourceShortDefinition: The aggregated time, in which temperatures below the lower boundary of the temperature range as defined in 'Temperature range idle state, lower boundary' are prevalent
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information on the time spent in extreme temperatures

label: Time Charging in High Temperature
sourceAttributeName: Time spent charging during extreme temperatures above boundary
definition: The cumulative seconds the battery has been actively charging while the temperature exceeded the upper operating boundary.
shortDefinition: The cumulative seconds the battery has been actively charging while the temperature exceeded the upper operating boundary.
sourceShortDefinition: The aggregated time, in which the battery is charged, while temperatures above the upper boundary of the temperature range as defined in 'Temperature range idle state, upper boundary' are prevalent
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information on the time spent charging in extreme temperatures
dinSpecRecommendation: Additionally, histograms should be used for the time spent charging in extreme temperatures, to keep the implementation effort and storage size lean. That means dividing the entire range of temperature values into a series of intervals and then accumulate the time spent in each interval.

label: Time Charging in Low Temperature
sourceAttributeName: Time spent charging during extreme temperatures below boundary
definition: The total seconds the battery spent charging while exposed to temperatures below the lower operating boundary.
shortDefinition: The total seconds the battery spent charging while exposed to temperatures below the lower operating boundary.
sourceShortDefinition: The aggregated time, in which the battery is charged, while temperatures below the lower boundary of the temperature range as defined in 'Temperature range idle state, lower boundary' are prevalent
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information on the time spent charging in extreme temperatures
dinSpecRecommendation: Additionally, histograms should be used for the time spent charging in extreme temperatures, to keep the implementation effort and storage size lean. That means dividing the entire range of temperature values into a series of intervals and then accumulate the time spent in each interval.

label: Deep Discharge Events
definition: The number of occurrences where the battery was discharged below the minimum safe voltage threshold.
shortDefinition: The number of occurrences where the battery was discharged below the minimum safe voltage threshold.
sourceShortDefinition: The number of occasions, in which voltage has dropped below the lower operational limit as provided in the battery passport
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must include information on the tracking of harmful events, such as the number of deep discharge events for stationary BESS and LMT batteries using a BMS.
dinSpecRecommendation: This information should be reported voluntarily also for other battery categories, i.e. EV or all industrial batteries with a BMS.

label: Overcharge Events
definition: The count of instances where the battery was charged beyond its maximum permitted voltage or state of charge.
shortDefinition: The count of instances where the battery was charged beyond its maximum permitted voltage or state of charge.
sourceShortDefinition: The number of occasions, in which voltage has increased above the upper operational limit as provided in the battery passport
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
dinSpecRecommendation:
As a non-mandatory data attribute the battery passport should include information on the number of overcharge events.

The information on number of overcharge events should relate to the battery item in analogy to deep discharge events.

The update interval for the number of overcharge events should be analogous to that of the number of deep discharge events.

label: Accident Information
definition: A URI linking to documented information about any accidents or adverse incidents involving the battery.
shortDefinition: A URI linking to documented information about any accidents or adverse incidents involving the battery.
sourceShortDefinition: Information on accidents affecting the battery, including when incorporated in equipment it is powering.
updateRequirement: depending on individual definition of "up-to-date" based on this data attribute and its use case. (Currently no clarification through official guidance or delegated act expected)
regulatoryRequirement: The battery passport must contain information and data resulting from its use such as accidents.
dinSpecRecommendation:
Identification of accidents can be based on measuring abrupt positive or negative acceleration above a corresponding absolute threshold value. An identified accident should be followed by an assessment of battery safety.

Reporting of information on accidents should consider requirements of personal data protection legislation, e.g. by an appropriate level of anonymization.
